perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the terms below shall have the following meanings: (a) “Confidential Information” shall mean: (i) with respect to NEW RIVER, information as defined generally in section l(a)(ii) following, as well as information, in any format, whether written, oral, visual, electronic, or otherwise, and whether commercial, technical, non-technical, or regulatory in nature, including, without limitation, data, know-how, formulae, processes, designs, sketches, photographs, plans, drawings, specifications, samples, reports, pricing information, studies, findings, inventions, ideas, materials, documents, lists, financial information, investments, information concerning current or proposed products, services or methods of manufacture, operation and/or business plans, whether or not originated by NEW RIVER, which relates to NEW RIVER’s proprietary polypeptide and polymer conjugate chemistry and NEW RIVER’s platform technology Carrierwave™, and processes and analytical techniques associated with these products/technologies and their successors. Where any Confidential Information is derived from the synthesis in a unique manner, or into a unique concept, of various elements that may be contained in the public domain, no part of the Confidential Information shall be deemed to be in the public domain or to be in SHIRE’s possession or to be thereafter acquired by SHIRE merely because it embraces information in the public domain or general information that SHIRE may thereafter acquire.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the terms below shall have the following meanings: (a) “Confidential Information” shall mean: (i) with respect to NEW RIVER, information as defined generally in section l(a)(ii) following, as well as information, in any format, whether written, oral, visual, electronic, or otherwise, and whether commercial, technical, non-technical, or regulatory in nature, including, without limitation, data, know-how, formulae, processes, designs, sketches, photographs, plans, drawings, specifications, samples, reports, pricing information, studies, findings, inventions, ideas, materials, documents, lists, financial information, investments, information concerning current or proposed products, services or methods of manufacture, operation and/or business plans, whether or not originated by NEW RIVER, which relates to NEW RIVER’s proprietary polypeptide and polymer conjugate chemistry and NEW RIVER’s platform technology Carrierwave™, and processes and analytical techniques associated with these products/technologies and their successors. Where any Confidential Information is derived from the synthesis in a unique manner, or into a unique concept, of various elements that may be contained in the public domain, all parts of the Confidential Information shall be deemed to be in the public domain and to be in SHIRE’s possession or to be thereafter acquired by SHIRE merely because it embraces information in the public domain or general information that SHIRE may thereafter acquire.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that ALL parts of New River's confidential information are deemed public domain if derived from public sources, this directly contradicts section 5(a), which allows exceptions if the information isn't generally known in the industry or enters the public domain through Receiving Party's omission. This insertion creates in-text contradiction because this clause is placed here but it goes against section 5(a) by completely undermining the exceptions where confidential information remains protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information shall not include information which the Receiving Party is able to demonstrate by competent proof: (a) is now or hereafter becomes, through no act or omission on the part of the Receiving Party, generally known or available within the industry, or is now or later enters the public domain through no act or omission on the part of the Receiving Party;
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' such that if it's derived from public domain sources, *all* parts are deemed public domain. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that information is *not* considered confidential if it becomes generally known, but makes an exception if that happens through no fault of the Receiving Party. The changed text eliminates this exception, creating a direct conflict and in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees not to remove any materials or tangible items containing any of the other party’s Confidential Information from the premises of the Disclosing Party without the Disclosing Party’s consent. Each Party agrees to comply with any and all terms and conditions that the Disclosing Party imposes upon approved removal of such materials or items, including, without limitation, that the removed materials or items must be returned by a certain date, and that no copies of the removed materials or items are to be made.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees to remove any materials or tangible items containing any of the other party’s Confidential Information from the premises of the Disclosing Party. Each Party agrees to comply with any and all terms and conditions that the Disclosing Party imposes upon approved removal of such materials or items, including, without limitation, that the removed materials or items must be returned by a certain date, and that copies of the removed materials or items are to be made.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "not to remove" to "to remove", it creates a contradiction. Now both parties are obligated to remove confidential material. Also it now implies, that each party must make copies of removed materials, which can cause security breaches in the confidential information. The original statement implies that they must not make copies. This goes against the Non-disclosure obligations written in Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees: (a) to hold the other Party’s Confidential Information in strict confidence;
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text stated that each party agrees 'not to remove' confidential information, while the changed text states that each party agrees 'to remove' confidential information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text: 'Each Party agrees: (a) to hold the other Party’s Confidential Information in strict confidence.' By requiring the removal of confidential information, the changed text makes it impossible to hold that information in strict confidence. Additionally, the change from 'no copies are to be made' to 'copies are to be made' also directly contradicts the obligation to hold information in strict confidence.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the Disclosing Party’s request, the Receiving Party will promptly return to the Disclosing Party all materials or tangible items containing the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information and all copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Disclosing Party’s request, the Receiving Party is not required to return to the Disclosing Party all materials or tangible items containing the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information and all copies thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The modification now implies the receiving party is not obligated to return confidential information. This creates uncertainty regarding ownership and control of the disclosed information. This contradicts section 3 Non-Disclosure Obligation where each party is to hold information in strict confidence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees: (a) to hold the other Party’s Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) to exercise at least the same care in protecting the other Party’s Confidential Information from disclosure as the Party uses with regard to its own Confidential Information; (c) not to disclose such Confidential Information to third parties; and Page 2 of 7 (d) not to use any Confidential Information for any purpose except for the Business Purpose without the prior, written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text states the Receiving Party 'will promptly return' confidential information, while the changed text says they 'are not required to return' it. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text which states that each party agrees 'to hold the other Party’s Confidential Information in strict confidence' and 'not to disclose such Confidential Information to third parties'. The changed text undermines the obligation to protect and return confidential information, creating a direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party recognizes and agrees that nothing contained in this Agreement will be construed as granting any rights to the Receiving Party, by license or otherwise, to use any of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information except as specified in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party recognizes and agrees that this Agreement grants full rights to the Receiving Party, by license or otherwise, to use any of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information even if not specified in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the agreement grants rights even if not specified, this directly contradicts the limitation specified in the original text where use is restricted to the terms of the agreement. This contradicts Section 3.(d) of the Non-Disclosure Obligation where the receiving party can't use the information for any other purpose than the Business Purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees: (d) not to use any Confidential Information for any purpose except for the Business Purpose without the prior, written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement grants full rights to use the disclosing party's confidential information even if not specified in the agreement. This directly contradicts the section stating that neither party can use confidential information for any purpose except for the business purpose without prior written consent. This fulfills the condition of a strong in-text contradiction as the changed text creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, making the terms unenforceable and creating confusion.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party acknowledges that all of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential information is owned solely by the Disclosing Party (and/or its licensors) and that the unauthorized disclosure or use of such Confidential Information would cause irreparable harm and significant injury, the degree of which may be difficult to ascertain and for which monetary damages would provide an insufficient remedy. Accordingly, each Party agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right to obtain an immediate injunction from any court of competent jurisdiction enjoining actual or threatened breach of this Agreement and/or actual or threatened disclosure of the Confidential Information without the necessity of posting a bond or other security. Each Party shall also have the right to pursue any other rights or remedies available at law or equity for such a breach.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party acknowledges that all of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential information may or may not be owned by the Disclosing Party (and/or its licensors) and that the unauthorized disclosure or use of such Confidential Information would not cause irreparable harm and significant injury, the degree of which may be difficult to ascertain and for which monetary damages would provide an insufficient remedy. Accordingly, each Party agrees that the Disclosing Party does not have the right to obtain an immediate injunction from any court of competent jurisdiction enjoining actual or threatened breach of this Agreement and/or actual or threatened disclosure of the Confidential Information without the necessity of posting a bond or other security. Each Party shall also have the right to pursue any other rights or remedies available at law or equity for such a breach.
perturbation.explanation: By adding uncertainty to the ownership, it now contradicts the legal relief section. It is now unclear if the disclosing party can seek legal relief if information is breached, due to the inserted "may or may not" and "does not have the right to obtain". This inserted contradiction affects the enforcement and ability to seek injunctive relief.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges that all of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential information may or may not be owned by the Disclosing Party (and/or its licensors) and that the unauthorized disclosure or use of such Confidential Information would not cause irreparable harm and significant injury, the degree of which may be difficult to ascertain and for which monetary damages would provide an insufficient remedy. Accordingly, each Party agrees that the Disclosing Party does not have the right to obtain an immediate injunction from any court of competent jurisdiction enjoining actual or threatened breach of this Agreement and/or actual or threatened disclosure of the Confidential Information without the necessity of posting a bond or other security. Each Party shall also have the right to pursue any other rights or remedies available at law or equity for such a breach.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Disclosing Party's information is owned solely by the Disclosing Party, and any breach would cause irreparable harm, entitling them to an immediate injunction. The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating the information 'may or may not be owned' and that a breach 'would not cause irreparable harm,' and that the disclosing party 'does not have the right to obtain an immediate injunction'. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, and therefore contradicts the original text. This directly contradicts the legal remedies initially available, creating a significant in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement, subject to the terms and conditions imposed on the removal of Confidential Information under paragraph 6, sets forth the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and may not be modified or amended except by written agreement executed by the parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement, subject to the terms and conditions imposed on the removal of Confidential Information under paragraph 6, is only a draft agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and may be modified or amended verbally by any party hereto.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "sets forth the entire agreement" to "is only a draft agreement", this introduces significant contradiction. The original text establishes the agreement as complete and binding unless modified in writing. The change states its a draft and can be verbally modified. This also goes against Section 17 where it must be a express or implied contract and not an verbal agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement, subject to the terms and conditions imposed on the removal of Confidential Information under paragraph 6, is only a draft agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and may be modified or amended verbally by any party hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'sets forth the entire agreement' with 'is only a draft agreement' and replaces 'may not be modified or amended except by written agreement executed by the parties hereto' with 'may be modified or amended verbally by any party hereto'. This directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement to be a complete and binding document modified only in writing. The Changed Text and Contradicted Text are the same, so this reinforces the point that there is indeed a contradiction because the document's statements contradict each other. This falls under the Structural Flaws definition.
